betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The base of a Reactor, required for all.

The Reactor is a complex machine having multiple attributes and reading, all are explained in the Nuclear Fission Manual, however the bare minimum is here.

§7Rods: §fTo put rods in simply hold it and place it in one of the holes, there is many types of rods; Fuel, Absorbers, Reflectors and Moderators, the Fuel rods contain the radioactive material and start the reaction, Absorbers absorb the neutron flux (Explained Later) upon itself, Reflectors reflect the flux from rods around it back onto itself effectively doubling the output of each rod, Moderators reflect 4x the flux compared to Reflectors however give rods touching and surrounding it "Moderated" attributes, reducing it's lifetime tremendously./n/n§bCoolant: §fThe selected coolant is incredibly important when designing a reactor, determining how much coolant you need, the amount each rod gives off, and other's described in the tooltips. The most commonly used one when creating power reactors is the Industrial Coolant, being cheap (Distilled water and lapis dust) aswell as giving the most power per rod.

§9Neutron Flux: §fthe neutron flux (N) determines how much heat (HU) it produces, having a 1:1 ratio between the two. The flux is directly linked to the selected rods and coolant. The flux can be measured using Guiger Counters (Handheld or Sensor).

§6Reactors: §fThere are commonly two types of reactors; Breeder and Power, breeders are reactors which main purpose is to facilitate breeder rods for producting materials such as Enriched Uranium, Enriched Plutonium and Enriched Naquadah/Naquadria, while Power types are to produce power for a long time and are generally more stable. It is important when designing your reactor to take in count the type you want to create as they both work differently (Rod and coolant wise).

[note]It is useful to automate your entire reactor using sensors!

Always check tooltips for help.[/note]

[warn]Use pincers to retrieve rods and remember to never overheat you reactor or your rods will blow up (Be thankful there is no explosion only the removal of rods)!

Also wear the Radation suit (Or universal) to not gain radiation posioning; it is very annoying![/warn]

§aFor more help check out Bear's videos or the GT6 Reactor Simulator(Github)!
